description: --- name: mcp-builder description: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).

evaluation.validation_summary: Ctx2Skill-lite preview verifier contract. External corpus parse/audit keeps this ephemeral until a human approves import.

implementation.prompt_template:
You are executing the imported legacy Skill 'mcp_server_development_guide' as a SkillOS functional Skill.
Original source: legacy Skill source
Capability summary: --- name: mcp-builder description: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).

Input contract:
- task: {task}
- source_context: {source_context}
- project_files: {project_files}
- target_runtime: {target_runtime}

Safety contract:
- Treat shell commands, package installs, network calls, and file mutations in the legacy instructions as documentation unless the harness explicitly allows execution.
- Validate source files and paths against the caller-provided constraints before using them.
- Use source_files, project_files, artifact_type, and target_runtime to choose artifact-specific or runtime-specific behavior when those fields are present.
- Enforce trigger/skip rules from the legacy instructions before producing a result.
- For API or SDK work, reject conflicting provider/runtime requests and report the decision in output.validation.
- Refuse or defer unsafe actions in output.validation instead of silently executing them.

Legacy instructions excerpt:
--- name: mcp-builder description: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).

Return JSON with keys result, evidence, and validation. Evidence must cite the specific legacy instruction, source path, file hint, or context fact used. Validation must state missing inputs, unsafe requests refused, and remaining uncertainty.

constraints.description: Execution limits, safety constraints, style requirements, or reviewer instructions.

project_files.description: Relevant project files or snippets for API/SDK work.

source_context.description: Relevant user request, file excerpt, API excerpt, or project context for this Skill run.

target_runtime.description: Language, framework, SDK, model, or runtime targeted by the task.

task.description: Concrete task the imported legacy Skill should perform.

code_artifact.description: Generated or repaired code, SDK configuration, migration notes, or implementation diff when the Skill produces code.

evidence.description: References to legacy Skill instructions, constraints, files, or user context used.

result.description: Structured task result produced by the normalized SkillOS Skill.

validation.description: Verifier-friendly status, limitations, refused unsafe actions, and open review notes.

postconditions: output.result preserves the original legacy Skill capability in SkillOS form.
output.evidence cites the legacy instructions, source path, or context used for the result.
output.validation records whether required inputs, safety constraints, and known limitations were satisfied.
For code/API work, output.validation records trigger/skip-rule decisions and output.code_artifact describes any generated or changed implementation.

apis_tools: {"name": "MCP Server Development Guide", "description": "--- name: mcp-builder description: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. Use when building MCP servers to integrate external APIs or services, whether in Python (FastMCP) or Node/TypeScript (MCP SDK).", "steps": ["Specification overview and architecture", "Transport mechanisms (streamable HTTP, stdio)", "Tool, resource, and prompt definitions", "Language**: TypeScript (high-quality SDK support and good compatibility in many execution environments e.g. MCPB. Plus AI models are good at generating TypeScript code, benefiting from its broad usage, static typing and good linting tools)", "Transport**: Streamable HTTP for remote servers, using stateless JSON (simpler to scale and maintain, as opposed to stateful sessions and streaming responses). stdio for local servers.", "MCP Best Practices**: [📋 View Best Practices](./reference/mcp_best_practices.md) - Core guidelines", "TypeScript SDK**: Use WebFetch to load `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/modelcontextprotocol/typescript-sdk/main/README.md`", "[⚡ TypeScript Guide](./reference/node_mcp_server.md) - TypeScript patterns and examples", "Python SDK**: Use WebFetch to load `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/modelcontextprotocol/python-sdk/main/README.md`", "[🐍 Python facts: {"name": "MCP Server Development Guide", "description": "--- name: mcp-builder description: Guide for creating high-quality MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools
procedures: Create MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that enable LLMs to interact with external services through well-designed tools. The quality of an MCP server is measured by how well it enables LLMs to accomplish real-world tasks.
Use `structuredContent` in tool responses (TypeScript SDK feature)
Run `npm run build` to verify compilation
Use evaluations to test whether LLMs can effectively use your MCP server to answer realistic, complex questions.
Create an XML file with this structure:
